breadth.note: NARROWED overnight vs the early-AM run: only 5 of 11 sectors now Leading on 1m RS (XLV +6.0, XLF +5.1, XLI +3.0, XLU +2.5, XLP +1.3) — down from 8 at 05:52 ET. XLY (+1.3→−0.5) and XLRE (+3.0→−0.6) rolled into Weakening; XLB (+1.1→−1.5) fell to Lagging. XLK/XLC/XLE remain Lagging. Defensive/financials leadership also SHRANK in magnitude (XLF +8.8→+5.1, XLV +9.6→+6.0, XLU +5.7→+2.5). Proxy from sector-member bars, not an exchange breadth feed.

regime.note: Regime shifted RISK-OFF-ward vs the prior calm read: vol EXPANDED (VIX 16.13→17.4 Tier-A; +~8% on 7/7 to ~17.5 — web-verified) on a chip-led Nasdaq selloff (−1.16%, SMH −3%, Samsung −7% on a beat-but-not-enough print) AND a geopolitical oil shock (WTI +4.3% to $73.43 after the US revoked Iranian crude licenses; 10y ~4.54–4.56%). Breadth NARROWED (8→5 Leading) and cyclicals/rate-sensitives (XLY/XLB/XLRE) rolled over — so this is defensive+financials-led de-risking, not broadening. BUT the VIX curve is still in CONTANGO (verified) = an orderly wobble, not a panic. Falsifier: IVTS breaking >1.0 (backwardation) flips this to genuine risk-off. Two-sided, with a fresh inflation/geopolitical tail into a rate-heavy week.

attention.title: Vol EXPANDED off the lows on a chip selloff + geopolitical oil shock — but the curve is still contango
attention.what: VIX 16.13→17.4 Tier-A (+1.27); jumped ~+8% on 7/7 to ~17.5 (web-verified) as the Nasdaq fell −1.16% (SMH −3%, Samsung −7% on a beat-that-missed-the-bar) and WTI spiked +4.3% to $73.43 after the US revoked Iranian crude export licenses. 10y ~4.54–4.56%. Term structure VERIFIED still CONTANGO (IVTS ~0.85, VIX3M ~19, day 62).
attention.why: This is the change that matters: the prior 'calm/positive-gamma compression' read is stale — vol is expanding on a twin catalyst (AI/chip valuation wobble + an inflationary oil shock). But contango says it's an orderly de-risk, not a panic. The line in the sand is the VIX curve: IVTS >1.0 (backwardation) would confirm a genuine risk-off regime shift; until then, treat pops as hedge-able, not capitulatory.

attention.title: Breadth NARROWED (8→5 Leading) — leadership collapsed back to pure defensives + financials
attention.what: Leading sectors dropped from 8 (05:52 ET) to 5: only XLV (+6.0), XLF (+5.1), XLI (+3.0), XLU (+2.5), XLP (+1.3) still lead on 1m RS. XLY (+1.3→−0.5) and XLRE (+3.0→−0.6) rolled to Weakening; XLB (+1.1→−1.5) fell to Lagging. Even the leaders SHRANK (XLF +8.8→+5.1, XLV +9.6→+6.0). XLK 1m RS actually improved (−6.2→−1.5) but is still Lagging. Est. from sector-member bars.
attention.why: Yesterday's 'broadening' has REVERSED into a narrower, defensively-led tape — cyclicals (XLY/XLB) and rate-sensitives (XLRE) rolling over is a risk-off tell, not healthy rotation. The pair trade is now XLF/XLV over XLK/XLC, but with shrinking conviction on the long side. Negate: XLK 1m RS crossing back above zero + cyclicals re-leading.

attention.title: Energy DIVERGENCE: XLE was 7/7's top sector on the oil spike, yet still the deepest RRG laggard
attention.what: XLE 1m RS −3.9, 3m RS −19.9 (worst cell, Lagging) — but energy was the best S&P sector on 7/7 (+~3%) as WTI jumped +4.3% to $73.43 on the revoked Iranian licenses (Occidental/Devon/APA +5–6%, web-verified). A one-day, crude/geopolitical-driven pop against a deeply negative trailing trend.
attention.why: This is a trap for trend-followers: the RRG says 'avoid', the tape says 'crude just gapped'. Actionable read — it's a geopolitical spike to fade for trend unless WTI HOLDS >$73 AND XLE 1m RS crosses positive; but energy is also the one clean hedge for the inflation/oil tail into CPI (7/14). Watch crude, not the RRG cell, for the next 1–2 sessions.

attention.title: Momentum split: 5-day cracked HARDER while 21-day quietly turned UP
attention.what: 5-day deteriorated further — SPMO −7.16 (weakest), VGT −4.62, QQQ −3.92 (all worse than the early-AM read) — yet 21-day IMPROVED: QQQ −4.1→+0.46, SPMO −2.08→+4.11, VGT −7.0→−0.99. 63-day still strong (SPMO +28.8, VGT +27.1, QQQ +20.3). MSFT the outlier: 5d +2.5, 21d still −8.3. Est. from watchlist bars.
attention.why: A sharp 5-day selloff sitting on a medium-term trend that is actually healing (21d up) = a shakeout, not (yet) a trend-break. The tell to trim SPMO/VGT is 21-day rolling back negative WITH the 5-day; right now they're diverging, so this is a 'don't add, don't panic-sell' zone. One factor unwind hits all three (SPMO/VGT/QQQ move together).

attention.title: Rate-heavy week: FOMC minutes + 10y auction TODAY; CPI 7/14 is the fork
attention.what: VERIFIED calendar: FOMC minutes (Jun 16–17 meeting) today 2:00pm ET + 10y note auction 1:00pm; jobless claims + 30y auction 7/9; June CPI 7/14 8:30am; June PPI 7/15 8:30am; monthly OPEX 7/17. 10y ~4.54–4.56% into supply (web-verified 7/7).
attention.why: The de-risk is rate- AND oil-driven now. A weak 10y/30y auction (yields >4.5% into supply) or hawkish minutes tone re-prices the front end and pressures long-duration tech; the oil spike raises headline-CPI risk into 7/14. This is the near-term decision cluster — the far-out marquee (7/29) is secondary this week.

attention.title: SPY levels are STALE (Jul-6) — and matter more now that VIX popped; re-pull before the open
attention.what: Last-known SPY structure (2026-07-06, STALE): flip ~740, call wall ~750, put wall ~720, ATH 760.4, IV-rank ~15. No fresh chain this run. With VIX +~8% on 7/7 and a shock in the tape, gamma positioning has likely shifted.
attention.why: The flip is the calm/violent line: above it dealers dampen (grind), below it flips to negative-gamma acceleration — and a vol pop pulls spot toward that line. These 2-day-old levels are the least trustworthy block in the brief right now; re-pull the chain in the gamma lab before sizing anything off them.

attention.title: MSFT −1.7% into a Jul-29 FOMC-day print; counter-trend, not a trend-turn
attention.what: MSFT $382.24 (−1.7% vs the 388.84 early-AM read); 5d +2.5 (only positive 5-day in the watchlist) but 21d −8.3 and 63d just +2.9. A diverged laggard, giving back its bounce, into a binary print that lands the SAME DAY as the FOMC decision (7/29). Est. from bars.
attention.why: Short-term bounce fading against a deteriorated medium-term trend frames MSFT as an event trade, not a momentum long — and the 7/29 FOMC+earnings collision compounds realized vol. The margin/capex guide is the fork (re-rate vs retest the $350s).

recommendations.subject: Favor XLF + XLV over XLK / XLC; trim the concentrated tech tail (VGT / SPMO) into any bounce
recommendations.rationale: The tape's pair trade, but with SHRINKING conviction: XLK is still Lagging (1m RS −1.5, improving from −6.2) while XLF (1m RS +5.1) and XLV (1m RS +6.0) still lead — yet leadership magnitudes compressed (XLF +8.8→+5.1, XLV +9.6→+6.0) and breadth narrowed 8→5 Leading. Overweight XLF/XLV vs XLK/XLC until XLK 1m RS crosses back above zero (the negate) OR the leaders keep bleeding RS (then the whole tape is de-risking, hedge instead). VGT/SPMO carry the de-gross risk and printed the weakest 5-day. Estimate from RRG + momentum-Δ, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Trim cyclicals/rate-sensitives (XLY, XLB, XLRE) — they just rolled out of Leading
recommendations.rationale: Change-detection trigger: XLY (+1.3→−0.5) and XLRE (+3.0→−0.6) dropped to Weakening and XLB (+1.1→−1.5) fell to Lagging in one session — the cyclical wing of yesterday's 'broadening' is unwinding. In a rising-VIX, oil-shock tape that skews risk-off; reduce cyclical/REIT exposure, keep defensives. Re-add on XLY/XLB 1m RS crossing back >0. Estimate from sector RS, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Energy (XLE): DON'T chase the geopolitical oil pop for trend — but it's the inflation-tail hedge
recommendations.rationale: XLE was 7/7's top S&P sector (+~3%, WTI +4.3% to $73.43 on revoked Iranian licenses) yet its trailing RRG is the worst cell (1m −3.9, 3m −19.9, Lagging). Trend-entry trigger: WTI HOLDS >$73 AND XLE 1m RS crosses positive — until both, treat it as a crude/geopolitical spike to fade, not a trend turn. Separately, a long-energy or crude tilt is the cleanest hedge for the oil-driven headline-CPI (7/14) risk. Estimate, not advice.

recommendations.subject: Momentum complex (SPMO / VGT / QQQ) — this is a shakeout, not yet a trend-break; don't add, don't panic-sell
recommendations.rationale: 5-day cracked harder (SPMO −7.2, VGT −4.6, QQQ −3.9) but 21-day quietly turned UP (QQQ +0.5, SPMO +4.1) and 63-day still holds (+28.8 / +27.1 / +20.3). The actionable trim trigger is 21-day rolling back NEGATIVE alongside the 5-day (they're diverging now, so hold). Reclaim of QQQ's 50-day + XLK RS turning up = the all-clear. Deep-link the momentum lab for the live crossover.

recommendations.subject: Carry a defined hedge into June CPI (Jul 14) — vol popped but the curve is still contango, so premium isn't panicked yet
recommendations.rationale: The de-risk is now rate- AND oil-driven; the +4.3% WTI spike raises headline-CPI risk, and CPI 7/14 8:30am is the near-term binary. VIX 17.4 with term structure VERIFIED still in contango (IVTS ~0.85) = protection has repriced but not yet into backwardation/panic pricing — a reasonable window to add a defined-risk hedge before the print. SPY flip (~740, STALE) is the reference; re-pull the chain first. Estimate, not advice.

recommendations.subject: MSFT into Jul 29 (FOMC-day print)
recommendations.rationale: Counter-trend +2.5% 5-day pop now fading (−1.7% to $382.24) with medium-term momentum still negative (21d −8.3) — and the print lands the SAME day as the FOMC decision, doubling the volatility. Event trade, not a momentum long. Contrarian and two-sided; size for the double catalyst.

psychology.read: REGIME: late-cycle de-risk with a FRESH vol + geopolitical catalyst — no longer the 'calm/positive-gamma' read of the prior run. NAME it: an orderly-but-expanding-vol regime (VIX 16.13->17.4 Tier-A; +~8% on 7/7 to ~17.5, web-verified) driven by a twin shock — an AI/chip valuation wobble (Nasdaq -1.16%, SMH -3%, Samsung beat-but-not-enough) AND an inflationary oil spike (WTI +4.3% to $73.43 on revoked Iranian licenses). The VIX curve is still in CONTANGO (IVTS ~0.85, day 62 — verified), so this is a controlled de-risk, not a panic. CROWD POSTURE: was complacent (Dow at records intraday 7/7) and is now hedging — breadth NARROWED (8->5 Leading), leadership collapsed back to pure defensives (XLV/XLP/XLU) + financials (XLF), and cyclicals/REITs (XLY/XLB/XLRE) rolled over. Reaction asymmetry still punishes tech misses (sell-the-news; Samsung's beat was sold). FALSIFIERS: (1) VIX term structure inverting to BACKWARDATION (IVTS >1.0) = the calm/orderly read breaks into genuine risk-off; (2) XLK 1m RS crossing back >0 + QQQ reclaiming its 50-day = the de-risk is over; (3) WTI mean-reverting back under $70 removes the inflation/energy tail and de-fangs the 7/14 CPI headline risk.
inputs: VIX 16.13->17.4 Tier-A (vol EXPANSION); +~8% on 7/7 to ~17.5 (web-verified) — reverses the prior compression read
VIX term structure CONTANGO (IVTS ~0.85, VIX3M ~19, day 62 — web-verified) = orderly, not panic; falsifier is IVTS >1.0
breadth NARROWED: 8->5 sectors Leading on 1m RS; XLY/XLRE->Weakening, XLB->Lagging; leaders' RS magnitudes also shrank (proxy)
chip-led selloff (web-verified 7/7): Nasdaq -1.16%, SMH -3%, Samsung -7% on a beat-that-missed-the-bar; AI-capex 'show-me' skepticism
geopolitical oil shock (web-verified 7/7): WTI +4.3% to $73.43 after the US revoked Iranian crude licenses; energy top S&P sector +~3%
10y ~4.54-4.56% into 10y/30y supply this week (web-verified); rate + oil = twin pressure on long-duration tech
momentum split: 5-day cracked harder (SPMO -7.2) while 21-day turned UP (QQQ +0.5, SPMO +4.1) — shakeout, not (yet) trend-break
MSFT $382.24 (-1.7%), 5d +2.5 fading vs 21d -8.3 (counter-trend) into a 7/29 FOMC-day print
Fear&Greed NOT fetched this run — sentiment band unknown, not assumed
psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: CPI (7/14) odds shaded so hot+in-line > cool: the crowd is positioned FOR continued de-risk AND the 7/7 oil spike biases headline upward, so an in-line/hot print confirms the trade while a cool print is the squeeze. MSFT miss shaded slightly heavier than the (stale) option-implied for sell-the-news asymmetry, compounded by the 7/29 FOMC collision. OPEX pin nudged 0.55->0.52 because VIX popped off the lows (less certain positive-gamma) and CPI/PPI/oil can knock spot off the 740 pin. All labeled estimates seeded from STALE options (Jul-6) / unverified consensus — re-seed live.

QQQ.status: $707.5; 5d −3.9, 21d +0.5 (turned positive), 63d +20.3. Long trend intact and the 21-day is quietly healing even as the 5-day cracks — a shakeout, not a trend-break. Counter-trend until XLK relative-strength turns up.

SPMO.status: $149.98; 5d −7.2 (weakest in the watchlist) but 21d +4.1 and 63d +28.8 (strongest). The purest momentum/semis long — hit hardest by the chip selloff, but its medium+long trend is the healthiest. First to reward if the shakeout resolves up.

VGT.status: $114.0; 5d −4.6, 21d −1.0, 63d +27.1. Highest-beta tech proxy sitting in the Lagging sector (XLK); took the SMH −3% chip hit. The concentrated tech tail — falls hardest on a de-gross.

MSFT.status: $382.24; 5d +2.5 (only positive 5-day, but fading from +5.5) yet 21d -8.3, 63d +2.9. Counter-trend bounce giving back into the Jul-29 print — which lands the SAME day as the FOMC decision (double catalyst). Medium-term momentum still negative. Event trade, not a momentum long.

experimental.title: VIX term-structure regime flag: contango→backwardation cross (idea)
experimental.note: Method: track IVTS = VIX/VIX3M (and VIX9D/VIX) daily; flag the cross of IVTS through 1.0 (contango→backwardation) as a regime-change trigger, with day-count of the current regime and a base-rate study of forward SPX returns/realized-vol after each cross. This run's whole read hinges on the curve STAYING in contango (IVTS ~0.85, day 62 — verified); backwardation is the named falsifier. Inputs: ^VIX/^VIX9D/^VIX3M series (add VIX9D/3M to rlData). No owning tool watches the curve cross today. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Crude/geopolitical shock → energy RRG divergence monitor (idea)
experimental.note: Method: flag when a sector is a top-1/2 daily performer while its trailing RRG cell is deeply Lagging (this run: XLE +~3% on WTI +4.3% yet 1m RS -3.9 / 3m -19.9) — a one-day event vs trend divergence — then track whether the daily pop leads the RS (trend turn) or fades (spike). Overlay WTI level + its 5/20-day change on XLE relative-strength; trigger 'trend-follow' only when crude HOLDS a level AND 1m RS crosses >0. Inputs: XLE + sector daily bars (have), CL/WTI series (need to add). Recurs whenever a geopolitical oil move hits a lagging energy tape. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Rate-driven rotation gauge: XLK-vs-XLF RS overlaid on the 10y yield (idea)
experimental.note: Method: plot XLK/XLF (and XLK/XLV) relative-strength ratio against the 10y Treasury yield and its 5/20-day change; regress sector RS-momentum on d(10y) to quantify how rate-sensitive the current leadership rotation is, and flag when the correlation breaks (rotation losing its rate driver = possible tech re-entry). Inputs: XLK/XLF/XLV daily bars (have), ^TNX or 10y yield series (need to add to rlData). This run's thesis is explicitly rate+oil-driven — no owning tool ties sector RS to yields today. Promote if recurring.

experimental.title: Double-catalyst vol event-study: FOMC-day + mega-cap earnings collision (idea)
experimental.note: Method: event-study base rates for days when an FOMC decision and a top-10 S&P name's earnings land together (Jul 29 = FOMC + MSFT) — measure realized-vs-implied vol, average |move|, and next-day drift vs single-catalyst days. Inputs: historical FOMC dates + mega-cap earnings dates + SPX/QQQ realized vol + straddle-implied. Quantifies the compounding this run flags qualitatively. Not yet built; promote if the pattern recurs across quarters.
